# Stats view

## {.sidebar}

```{ojs}
viewof themes_new = Inputs.radio(
  ["demography", "education", "housing" , "labour market" , "mobility" , "health"],
  {
    value: "demography",
    label: "themes"
  }
)
```


```{ojs}
//| output: false
city_data = FileAttachment("city_stats.csv").csv({ typed: true })
```


```{ojs}
//| output: false

filtered_new = city_data
  .filter(city => themes_new.includes(city.themes))
  .map(city => ({ ...city, year: String(Math.trunc(city.year)) }))

Reactable.setData('tbl-new', filtered_new)
```

## Column

### Row

```{r}

library(leaflet)
swiss_cities <- data.frame(
  city = c("Winterthur", "Zurich", "Berne", "Biel/Bienne",
           "Lucerne", "Basel", "Lugano", "Lausanne", "Geneva"),
  lat = c(47.5000, 47.3769, 46.9480, 47.1368,
          47.0502, 47.5596, 46.0037, 46.5197, 46.2044),
  lon = c(8.7333, 8.5417, 7.4474, 7.2468,
          8.3093, 7.5886, 8.9511, 6.6323, 6.1432)
)

swiss_cities %>% 
leaflet() %>% 
  setView(lng = 8, lat = 46.8, zoom = 7) %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addCircleMarkers(~lon, ~lat, radius = 10)


```

### Row 

```{ojs}
Inputs.table(filtered_new)
```


## Column

### Row 

```{ojs}
Plotly = require("https://cdn.plot.ly/plotly-2.27.0.min.js")

treemap = {
  const values = filtered_new.map(d => d.statistics)
  const labels = filtered_new.map(d => d.cities)
  const parents = filtered_new.map(d => d.themes)

  const trace = {
    type: "treemap",
    values: values,
    labels: labels,
    parents: parents,
    textinfo: "label+value+percent parent"
  }

  const div = document.createElement("div")
  div.style.width = "100%"
  div.style.height = "100%"

  const layout = {
    autosize: true,
    height: window.innerHeight * 0.8,
    margin: { t: 0, l: 0, r: 0, b: 0 }
  }

  Plotly.newPlot(div, [trace], layout, { responsive: true })
  return div
}
```
